decisions.decision: Batch overnight execution with auto-approved UAT
decisions.rationale: User explicitly authorized: 'let's say it's all approved, I'll check in the morning'. SUMMARYs explicitly marked UAT as 'pre-approved batch — pending in-browser confirmation' to preserve audit truth.

decisions.decision: Sequential mode (no worktree isolation) for all 5 plans
decisions.rationale: Windows + worktree + checkpoint plans is a known footgun (#2070 SUMMARY.md stranding). Each wave had only 1 plan, so worktree gave no parallelism benefit anyway.

decisions.decision: D-10 used PATTERNS.md Candidate A (.task-grid-rail overflow-x: hidden)
decisions.rationale: Could not run live DevTools diagnosis during overnight batch. Candidate A is highest-probability cause per pattern map. Documented assumption in 86.3-03-SUMMARY.md so morning UAT can confirm or trigger Candidate B/C investigation.

decisions.decision: D-11 used Root Cause A (cache floorX in scroll-clamp closure)
decisions.rationale: Could not run live Performance trace during overnight batch. Root Cause A addresses the largest new risk this phase introduces (the new scroll listener doing O(n) work per scroll event). Documented assumption in 86.3-04-SUMMARY.md.

Code review caught H-01 (month-view label format always included startMMM, contradicting CONTEXT.md D-03 examples) and H-02 (Invalid Date guard never fired because new Date(undefined) is truthy). Both would fail morning UAT — fixing pre-emptively avoids burning user time on obvious bugs. Defended under user's batch authorization.

decisions.decision: Saved 'overnight batch execution' as feedback memory
decisions.rationale: User's pattern of authorizing 'let's say approved, check in morning' is a real workflow preference distinct from default interactive UAT. Memorialized so future phases recognize it without re-asking.

Mark each test result inline. Tests 26 (H-01 label format), 13 (D-10 scrollbar), 21 (D-11 perf trace) are highest-uncertainty items — start there if time-constrained.
context_notes: Phase 86.3 fully implemented at the code level — verifier passed 9/9 must_haves. The phase is functionally shipped pending in-browser UAT. ROADMAP shows it as [x] complete with date 2026-05-06 (the gsd-tools phase complete command stamped that date even though today is 2026-05-07; minor cosmetic issue, ignorable). 19 commits added since 1de4dd1 across 2 source files (project-plan.js +250 lines, views.css +80 lines). All planning artifacts (PLANs, PATTERNS, SUMMARYs, VERIFICATION, REVIEW, HUMAN-UAT) committed. Branch v3.3, clean tree, no push. If morning UAT exposes failures, the standard /gsd-plan-phase 86.3 --gaps cycle handles gap closure. The two unverified diagnostic assumptions (D-10 Candidate A, D-11 Root Cause A) are the most likely sources of UAT failure — their SUMMARYs document the fallback investigation paths.
